overview.title: Health-Check REST API
overview.purpose: This project provides a simple REST API for checking the health status of a server. It is intended for system administrators and monitoring tools to quickly assess server status.

components.name: API Server
components.description: Serves a single health endpoint that responds to health check requests.
components.interface_summary: Operates using Express framework and exposes a single GET endpoint at /health that returns a JSON object with status information.

api_reference.description: Checks the health status of the server and returns it.
errors: None, as per specification acceptance criteria.

steps: Clone the repository containing the project files.
Run 'npm install' to install necessary dependencies.
Execute 'node src/server.js' to start the server.
Access the health check endpoint by navigating to 'http://localhost:3000/health' in a web browser or curl command.

operations.health_check: Access the GET /health endpoint. A 200 status code with JSON body {"status": "ok"} indicates the server is running correctly.
operations.logging: Logs are printed to the console including server start confirmation: 'health api on 3000'.

common_issues.cause: Port 3000 is in use or Node.js is not installed
common_issues.fix: Ensure Node.js is installed and no other application is using port 3000.
Start the server by running 'node src/server.js'.

documentation.summary: This documentation package describes the Health-Check REST API designed for quick server health assessments. It covers API endpoint details, quickstart instructions, operations guide, and known limitations, aimed at system administrators and developers.
